title: Finite-Temperature Spin Exchange-Correlation Kernel of the Uniform Electron Gas
authors: Pengcheng Hou, Zhiyi Li, Youjin Deng, Kun Chen
abstract: The finite-temperature spin response of the uniform electron gas (UEG) is a fundamental reference for spin-polarized and magnetized electron liquids, including warm dense matter (WDM), yet it remains far less constrained than charge response. Using variational diagrammatic Monte Carlo, we compute the static spin exchange--correlation (XC) kernel $K_{xc}(q;T)$ of the unpolarized UEG at metallic densities across the quantum-degenerate, warm-dense, and classical regimes. The kernel connects smoothly to zero-temperature spin-response parametrizations at low temperature, while heating suppresses the Fermi-surface-scale spin-correlation structure and weakens the XC-driven Stoner enhancement. Its long-wavelength limit provides a direct response test of the spin stiffness implied by thermal local-spin-density-approximation (LSDA) parametrizations, showing low-temperature consistency while exposing a resolved warm-dense residual in current LSDA parametrizations. In the classical regime, the spin XC kernel becomes nearly local on the Fermi-momentum scale, in sharp contrast to the corresponding charge XC kernel. These results provide a first-principles basis for finite-temperature spin-response theory and magnetized WDM modeling.
summary: This paper uses variational diagrammatic Monte Carlo to provide the first-principles spin exchange-correlation kernel for the uniform electron gas across a wide temperature range. It identifies discrepancies in current warm-dense matter models and demonstrates how thermal effects lead to a transition toward a local, momentum-independent spin response.
main_problem: The finite-temperature spin response of the uniform electron gas (UEG) is poorly constrained, particularly regarding the momentum-resolved spin exchange-correlation kernel across quantum-degenerate, warm-dense, and classical regimes.
main_result: The study provides first-principles data for the spin XC kernel, revealing that heating suppresses spin-correlation structures and Stoner enhancement, and identifying a 'warm-dense residual' error in current LSDA parametrizations.
method: Variational diagrammatic Monte Carlo (VDMC) using a screened-interaction formulation and the Principle of Minimal Sensitivity (PMS) to ensure convergence.
model_or_system: A 3D unpolarized uniform electron gas (UEG) at metallic densities, covering regimes from quantum-degenerate to classical temperatures.
key_observables: Static spin exchange-correlation kernel Kxc(q;T), spin stiffness (alpha_xc), Stoner enhancement, and the spin-interpolation exponent (p*).
important_parameters: Wigner-Seitz radius (rs) between 1 and 4, reduced temperature (theta = T/TF) from 0.25 to 8, and momentum transfer (q/kF).
main_assumptions: The use of a Yukawa-type screening scale for the VDMC stability and the reliance on the PDW spin-interpolation ansatz for evaluating the spin-polarization dependence.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the dimensionless spin XC kernel for various densities; Figure 2 compares spin stiffness and kernel values against LSDA models (GDB, corrKSDT); Supplemental figures show VDMC convergence and the temperature-driven flattening of the kernel.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of spin-response constraints, describes the VDMC methodology and convergence, presents results for the spin XC kernel across temperature and density, benchmarks these results against existing LSDA models, and discusses the high-temperature classical limit.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Static spin exchange-correlation (XC) kernel K− xc(q; θ) NF of the 3D unpolarized uniform electron gas (UEG) at Wigner-Seitz radius rs = 1 (a), 2 (b), and 4 (c) for reduced temperature θ = T/TF from 1/16 to 8, with the Fermi temperature TF and the zero-T density of states NF = kF /(2π2) (kF : Fermi wavevector). Color encodes θ, from low temperature (dark blue) to high temperature (dark red); see right-side legend. Open colored markers with thin connecting lines (guides to the eye): variational diagrammatic Monte Carlo (VDMC), this work. Filled navy circles: low-temperature VDMC data (θ = 0.04) from Chen and Haule [26]. Black solid and dashed lines: zero-T Kaplan–Kukkonen (KK) [14]...

FIG. 2. Top panel: dimensionless q = 0 spin XC kernel −K− xc(0; θ) NF at rs = 1, 2, 3, 4; the minus sign aligns Stoner enhancement with the upward direction (K− xc < 0 in the metallic-density window). Solid lines: GDB fit [21]; dashed lines: corrKSDT fit [19, 20]. Open symbols: VDMC data; the θ = 0.04 points are from Ref. [26], all other points are from this work. Bottom panel: relative deviations ∆K−/|K− GDB|, in percent, with ∆K−= K−−K− GDB for each VDMC point or corrKSDT curve.

FIG. 3. Normalized static XC kernels at rs = 1: (a) K+ xc(q; θ)/K+ xc(0; θ) and (b) K− xc(q; θ)/K− xc(0; θ) vs q/kF for the matched temperature grid θ ∈{0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8}. Dashed horizontal line in (b): classical Curie asymptote K− xc(q; θ)/K− xc(0; θ) →1 at θ →∞. For θ ≳4, the spin ratio is within ∼10% of unity over q ≤2kF , whereas the charge ratio remains strongly q-dependent and is ≃0.56 at q = 2kF for θ = 8.
